definition_en: A drug used to prevent pain during surgery
example: She was given a general anaesthetic before the operation.

definition_en: The amount of medicine to be taken at one time
example: Follow the recommended dosage carefully.

definition_en: A medicine that kills or inhibits bacteria
example: The doctor prescribed antibiotics for the infection.

definition_en: A substance that stimulates immunity to a disease
example: The flu vaccine is available every autumn.

definition_en: An unintended effect of a medicine in addition to the main effect
example: Nausea is a common side effect of this medication.

definition_en: Describing a disease or condition that lasts for a long time
example: She manages a chronic condition with regular medication.

definition_en: A research study to test a new medical treatment on people
example: The new drug is currently in a clinical trial.

definition_en: The removal of a small piece of tissue for examination
example: The doctor ordered a biopsy to check for cancer.

definition_en: A medical image of the inside of the body using X-ray or MRI
example: He had an MRI scan after the accident.

definition_en: An operation to replace a damaged organ with a healthy one
example: She received a kidney transplant last year.

definition_en: A medicine that reduces pain
example: Take a painkiller if the headache persists.

definition_en: A widespread occurrence of a disease in a community
example: An epidemic of flu swept through the country.

definition_en: A reaction of the immune system to a harmless substance
example: She has a severe allergy to penicillin.

definition_en: Treatment of injury or disease through physical exercises and massage
example: After the operation she needed six weeks of physiotherapy.

definition_en: A hormone that controls blood sugar levels, used to treat diabetes
example: Diabetic patients inject insulin daily.

definition_en: A physical or mental sign that suggests a disease or condition
example: Fever and cough are symptoms of COVID-19.

definition_en: Protected against a disease because of having had it or been vaccinated
example: Once vaccinated, you become immune to the disease.

definition_en: A room or section in a hospital for patients with a similar condition
example: He was moved to the cardiac ward for observation.

definition_en: Taking too much of a medicine or drug, which can be dangerous
example: She was hospitalised after an accidental overdose.

definition_en: Permission given for medical treatment
example: Patients must give written consent before surgery.
